metadata.title: Data Space
metadata.description: Explanation of what a data space is

header.title: What is a data space?
header.lead: A data space is a collaborative environment where different organizations can securely, voluntarily, and in a governed way share information to generate shared value from data.

definition.title: Definition
definition.text: A data space is a digital environment that enables multiple organizations to securely share and manage data, respecting each participant's sovereignty and ensuring control over information.

importance.title: Why is it key for data-driven innovation?
importance.paragraph1: Unlike traditional models of information exchange, data spaces allow each participant to retain sovereignty over their digital assets, deciding who can access them, for what purpose, and under what conditions.
importance.paragraph2: Data spaces eliminate information silos, foster trust between organizations, and enable the development of new services, research, and artificial intelligence models without participating entities losing control over their data.

sovereignty.title: Data sovereignty
sovereignty.description: Each participant retains control over their data and decides how it is used.

interoperability.title: Interoperability
interoperability.description: Data can be shared between different systems and organizations using common standards.

governance.title: Federated governance
governance.description: Usage rules are transparent, auditable, and agreed upon by participants.

trust.title: Trust and traceability
trust.description: All operations are recorded and can be verified at any time.

value.title: Shared value creation
value.description: Participants collaborate to create knowledge, innovation, and new services.
